design_philosophy.core_loop: Observe opponent attack patterns → dodge/block → counter-punch during openings → build power meter → unleash knockout punches when opponent is dizzied
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Pattern recognition under time pressure with stamina management; memorizing opponent tells vs. reactive adaptation
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Rising boxer climbing ranks to become WVBA champion by studying and outsmarting increasingly skilled opponents
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strictly pattern-based boxing where reflexes matter less than observation; opponents have fixed, learnable tells rather than AI adaptation. Success comes from studying enemy behavior, not button-mashing. Three-round matches (time limit) create strategic pacing distinct from arcade fighter conventions.

difficulty.lives_system: Stamina meter with three-knockdown rule (TKO on third knockdown); KO if stamina fully depleted and cannot stand within 10-count
difficulty.checkpointing: Circuit-based progression (Minor Circuit → Major Circuit → World Circuit → Special Circuit); must defeat all opponents in each circuit before advancing
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Free Match Mode allows training against any of 16 opponents without circuit progression; players can practice specific matchups to learn patterns before circuit runs

progression.upgrade_system: Power meter accumulation during match (fills as player lands punches, enables knockout punches); Power-Up status (background color shift blue→green→yellow→red) increases punch speed/power but lost on knockdown
progression.unlock_mechanism: Complete each circuit's opponents sequentially to unlock next circuit; Special Circuit unlocks after World Circuit completion (gated by anti-piracy checkpoint)
replayability_hooks: Free Match Mode with two-player option for training/versus play
Opponent pattern variations encourage replaying to master each fighter
Power-Up status mechanics reward skilled pattern execution

world_building_techniques: Opponent character designs convey nationality/stereotype (Dragon Chan, Bald Bull, Mr. Sandman, Super Macho Man)
Circuit names (Minor/Major/World/Special) establish climbing champion hierarchy
Third-person behind-the-back camera perspective creates immersion as rising boxer

boss_design.philosophy: Each opponent is a unique pattern puzzle with telegraphed attack openings and defensive guard positions. Bosses are not harder versions of basic enemies—they are entirely distinct encounter designs. Success requires memorization of tells (specific animations that signal attacks) and positioning to hit open spots in opponent's guard.
notable_bosses: Dragon Chan - kicks as primary attack, requires dodge mechanics
Bald Bull - signature move requires specific counter-timing
Mr. Sandman - initial champion with complex guard patterns
Super Macho Man - late-circuit opponent with aggressive combinations
Unnamed Special Circuit opponents - highest difficulty with intricate pattern sets

faction_indicators: Circuit rankings establish social hierarchy (Minor → Major → World → Special)
Opponent nationality/character archetypes create implicit factions (international boxing associations)
Champion status progression signals player rank in community
danger_signals: Special Circuit access locked behind anti-piracy checks (system distinguishes legitimate vs. copied carts)
Cartridge header tampering triggers 'Copy Machine!?' error states
Multiple boot state tests identify unauthorized backup hardware
environmental_storytelling: Boxing ring setting consistent across all matches
Opponent cutscene intros (via Charles Martinet voice acting) establish personality/rivalry
Circuit progression through boxing federation hierarchy visualized through menu structure
Stamina/power meter displays create shared spectator language for versus play

sources_used: tcrf - Super Punch-Out!! (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor (debug functions, anti-piracy architecture, prototype differences)
wikipedia - Super Punch-Out!! (gameplay mechanics, control scheme, stamina system, character roster, development credits)
